title: Clematis coactilis
taxonomy: Clematis coactilis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the genus Clematis and the family Ranunculaceae. This plant belongs to the order Ranunculales and is native to Mexico. It is an evergreen climber with a compact growth habit and small, bell-shaped flowers. Clematis coactilis is a relatively rare and understudied species, with limited information available on its classification and characteristics. Further research is needed to fully understand its classification and relationships within the Clematis genus.
uses: Clematis coactilis has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Latin America, to treat various health conditions. Its roots are said to have anti-inflammatory properties and are used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory diseases. The plant is also used to treat digestive issues such as diarrhea and constipation. Additionally, Clematis coactilis is believed to have antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating wounds and preventing infection. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Clematis coactilis for medicinal purposes.
